Cardmarket uses cookies and other related tools. Aside from required cookies, we also apply other types of cookies, but only if you consent to them. These cookies help us improve our product by analyzing user data and applying personalized functions. If you accept all cookies, we will transfer your data to our partners, who will aggregate this data with other website data about you. You will find further information on individual cookies in our Privacy Policy, where you can adjust your cookie settings. This is where you can withdraw your consent to the various types of cookies on the website. About Us

Set Information

Mew ex Is the Star of the Show

Pokémon Card 151 is the special set of 2023 and the first holiday set for the Scarlet and Violet block. This set is unique in that it pays homage to the original 151 Pokémon, evoking the nostalgic charm of the franchise's early years. This set is arranged in Pokedex order, taking fans on a journey from the iconic Bulbasaur at slot #001, all the way to the elusive Mew ex at #151. It's a chance for enthusiasts to relive the magic of the first generation, just as they encountered these creatures while exploring the Kanto region in the original games.

Mew ex is one of the best new cards in the set that has a brilliant Ability [Restart]. This Ability lets you draw until you have 3 cards in hand once during your turn, and with multiple Mew ex in play, you can be drawing a fair few cards each turn. Mew ex’s attack, [Genome Hacking] is incredibly fitting of the New Species Pokémon, allowing you to copy one of the attacks of the opponent’s Active Pokémon. This is very similar to Mew cards of the past, but the best part about this attack is the fact it just costs 3 Colorless Energy. This means the Pokémon can be easily splashed into almost any deck with any kind of Energy acceleration to help deal with problematic matchups by using your opponent’s attacks against them! You’ll be sure to see this Pokémon in Lugia VSTAR decks, Miraidon ex decks and maybe even some Lozt Zone Toolbox decks going forward!

Let's Meet the Formidable Charizard ex

With an impressive HP of 330, Charizard ex wields the attack [Resolute Wings] which starts at 60 damage and escalates to 160 when Charizard ex has any damage counters on it. This cost-effective move ensures that Charizard isn't penalized after taking a hit, even if energy cards are scarce. However, the card's true might lies in its second attack [Detonating Vortex]. With this, Charizard ex can unleash a massive 330 damage, but at the expense of discarding three Energy cards, potentially knocking out almost any Pokémon in the game! To help manage this expensive cost, this Charizard ex pairs incredibly well with 2 other Charizard cards we have in the current Standard format. Charizard from Pokémon GO, with the Fierce Burn Ability, and Charizard ex from Obsidian Flames to help cover its Grass-type weakness.

The Triumphant Return of Kadabra and Alakazam

The Pokémon Card 151 set also heralds the full evolution line of Alakazam! Emerging from the elusive Kadabra (marking its return after an astonishing 21-year absence as a Stage 1 Pokémon), Alakazam ex is a formidable Psychic-type Pokémon.

Alakazam’s first attack [Mind Jack], starts at 90 damage but is increased by 30 for each of the opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This kind of attack has always been powerful and will put players in difficult positions.  However, Alakazam's standout feature is its second attack, [Dimensional Manipulation]. This is the first time we’ve ever seen an attack like this, and while it deals only 120 damage, it can be used from the Bench. This means you can keep your main attacking threat protected on the bench, whilst leaving other 1 Prize Pokémon in the Active position for your opponent to deal with.
